UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile on the LocalBid Exchange platform, including personal information, reliability, and verification status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: User's email address, used for login and notifications.

username.description: Publicly displayed username of the user.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

locationLatitude.description: Geographic latitude of the user's primary location.

locationLongitude.description: Geographic longitude of the user's primary location.

reliabilityScore.description: A score reflecting the user's reliability based on their behavior (e.g., showing up, completing deals). Defaulted to a starting value, e.g., 100.

totalSales.description: The total number of sales completed by this user as a seller.

averageSellerRating.description: The average rating received by this user as a seller, from 1 to 5.

isIdVerified.description: Indicates whether the user has successfully completed ID verification and received a 'Verified' badge.

idVerificationStatus.description: The current status of the user's ID verification process.

registrationDate.description: Timestamp when the user registered their account.

ReliabilityScoreHistory.title: ReliabilityScoreHistory
ReliabilityScoreHistory.description: Records individual events and their corresponding score changes that contribute to a user's overall reliability score.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReliabilityScoreHistory event.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile whose reliability score was affected. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ReliabilityScoreHistory)

eventType.description: The type of event that occurred, influencing the reliability score.

scoreChange.description: The numeric change applied to the user's reliability score for this event (positive for good, negative for bad).

timestamp.description: The date and time when this reliability event occurred.

associatedEntityId.description: Optional: Reference to the ID of the entity (e.g., Transaction, UserReport) that this reliability event is associated with.

details.description: Optional: Additional details or notes about the reliability event.

SafeMeetingPoint.title: SafeMeetingPoint
SafeMeetingPoint.description: Defines pre-approved safe public locations recommended for buyers and sellers to meet for transactions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SafeMeetingPoint entity.

name.description: The common name of the safe meeting point (e.g., 'Central Police Station', 'Coffee Corner Mall').

address.description: The full physical address of the safe meeting point.

latitude.description: Geographic latitude of the safe meeting point.

longitude.description: Geographic longitude of the safe meeting point.

type.description: The category or type of the safe meeting point.

description.description: Optional: Additional descriptive details about the meeting point, such as specific entrance or availability.

is24Hours.description: Indicates if the location is accessible 24 hours a day.

IdVerification.title: IdVerification
IdVerification.description: Stores the details and status of a user's identity verification attempt.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ID verification record.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile attempting verification. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N IdVerification)

documentType.description: The type of identification document submitted by the user.

documentFrontImageUrl.description: URL to the uploaded image of the front side of the identification document.

documentBackImageUrl.description: Optional: URL to the uploaded image of the back side of the identification document.

selfieImageUrl.description: Optional: URL to the uploaded selfie image for liveness detection or face matching.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the verification request was submitted.

status.description: The current status of the ID verification request.

reviewDate.description: Optional: The date and time when the verification request was reviewed.

reviewerId.description: Optional: Reference to the internal admin user who reviewed this verification request.

rejectionReason.description: Optional: The reason for rejection if the status is 'rejected' or 'retake_required'.

ChatThread.title: ChatThread
ChatThread.description: Represents a conversation thread between two or more users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatThread entity.

participantIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs participating in this chat thread. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N ChatThread)

associatedListingId.description: Optional: Reference to a Listing if this chat thread is related to a specific product. (Relationship: Listing 1:N ChatThread)

createdAt.description: The date and time when the chat thread was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time when the chat thread was last updated (e.g., a new message was sent).

lastMessageId.description: Reference to the ID of the last message sent in this thread. (Relationship: ChatMessage 1:1 ChatThread)

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents an individual message exchanged between users within a chat thread, including anti-scam analysis results.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

chatThreadId.description: Reference to the ChatThread this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatThread 1:N ChatMessage)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who sent this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

receiverId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is the primary recipient of this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

messageContent.description: The actual text content of the chat message.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the message was sent.

readStatus.description: Indicates if the message has been read by the receiver.

scamStatus.description: The result of the anti-scam guard's analysis of the message content. 'clean' if no issues, 'warning' if potential scam detected, 'blocked' if severe scam attempt prevented.

scamDetectionDetails.description: Optional: Details about what triggered a 'warning' or 'blocked' scam status (e.g., detected keywords or patterns).

definition.description: Stores the profile data for a specific user, identified by their authentication UID. The `userId` wildcard ensures ownership. This document includes crucial fields like `reliabilityScore` and `isIdVerified`.
A subcollection under each user's profile to log events that affect their reliability score. The `userId` field is denormalized in each document for Authorization Independence.

Records a user's ID verification submission and its status. Owned by the user, with review capabilities for administrators. The `userId` field is denormalized in each document for Authorization Independence.
Stores details of pre-approved safe public meeting locations. These documents are publicly readable for all users, but writeable only by authorized administrators.

Stores metadata for a chat conversation, including all `participantIds`. This `participantIds` array (e.g., `['uid1', 'uid2']`) is critical for authorization and provides Authorization Independence for the thread itself, allowing rules to check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.participantIds`.

Individual messages within a `ChatThread`. To ensure Authorization Independence, each `ChatMessage` document MUST include a denormalized `threadParticipantIds` array (copied from the parent `ChatThread`'s `participantIds`). This allows security rules to evaluate access purely from the message document itself (e.g., `request.auth.uid in resource.data.threadParticipantIds`), eliminating the need for `get()` calls to the parent thread document.

A collection for Database Access Control (DBAC) where the document ID (`adminId`) corresponds to a user's UID, granting them administrative privileges. Used for Authorization Independence in rules (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/app_roles/admin/$(request.auth.uid))`).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for LocalBid Exchange is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and predictable access modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & Denormalization Strategy:**

1.  **User-Owned Data:** `UserProfile`, `ReliabilityScoreHistory`, and `IdVerification` are structured under `/users/{userId}/...` paths. This makes the `userId` wild-card (`request.auth.uid`) the inherent owner, enabling atomic security rules without `get()` calls to parent documents. For instance, `ReliabilityScoreHistory` documents explicitly contain `userId` in their data, and `IdVerification` documents also contain `userId`. This denormalization means a document's authorization can be determined solely from its own data or its path. 
2.  **Collaborative Data (Chat):** `ChatThread` documents (e.g., `/chatThreads/{threadId}`) contain a `participantIds` array. This array serves as the denormalized authorization context for multi-user access to that specific thread. To extend this independence to `ChatMessage`s (which are in a subcollection of `ChatThread`), each `ChatMessage` document will *also* denormalize the `participantIds` from its parent `ChatThread` into a field like `threadParticipantIds`. This ensures that access to any `ChatMessage` document can be authorized by checking `request.auth.uid` against `resource.data.threadParticipantIds` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `ChatThread` document within security rules. This makes creation, update, and deletion of messages atomic and independently verifiable. 
3.  **Global Data:** `SafeMeetingPoint` documents (e.g., `/safeMeetingPoints/{pointId}`) are globally readable. Administration of these points (writes) will rely on a separate, existence-based `app_roles/admin/{adminId}` collection for security rules, avoiding custom claims and `get()` calls on `UserProfile` for role checks.

**Querying for Authorization & QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

1.  **User-Specific Data:** For `/users/{userId}/reliabilityScoreHistory` and `/users/{userId}/idVerifications`, `list` operations are implicitly secured by the path (`userId` must match `request.auth.uid`).
2.  **Public Data:** `/safeMeetingPoints` is designed for public read/list access, making `list` operations inherently secure and efficient.
3.  **Collaborative Data (`ChatThread`, `ChatMessage`):** For `ChatThread` (e.g., `/chatThreads`), `list` operations by a user will require a query filter such as `where('participantIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid)`. Firebase security rules can then enforce this `array-contains` filter, ensuring only relevant threads are returned to the requesting user (QAP compliant). Similarly, for `ChatMessage`s, a `list` operation will be restricted to messages where `request.auth.uid` is present in the denormalized `threadParticipantIds` field.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**

*   **Private User Data:** `/users/{userId}` for `UserProfile` follows the path-based ownership for private data.
*   **User-Owned Subcollections:** `/users/{userId}/reliabilityScoreHistory` and `/users/{userId}/idVerifications` extend the user-owned hierarchy.
*   **Collaborative Data:** `chatThreads/{threadId}` with its `participantIds` array, and `chatThreads/{threadId}/messages/{messageId}` (with denormalized `threadParticipantIds` in each message) provide a robust structure for multi-user interactions.
*   **Global Public Data:** `safeMeetingPoints/{pointId}` offers a segregated collection for publicly accessible, admin-managed data.
*   **DBAC (Database Access Control):** A dedicated `/app_roles/admin/{adminId}` collection is used to manage administrative privileges, relying on document existence for role-based authorization rather than custom claims, adhering to DBAC principles.
